name: pubmed-database
description: Direct REST API access to PubMed. Advanced Boolean/MeSH queries, E-utilities API, batch processing, citation management. For Python workflows, prefer biopython (Bio.Entrez). Use this for direct HTTP/REST work or custom API implementations.
capabilities: Construct advanced Boolean queries with field tags and operators
Search using MeSH terms and controlled vocabulary
Access PubMed programmatically via E-utilities REST API
Find articles by author, journal, PMID, or DOI
Build systematic review search strategies using PICO framework
Filter results by publication type, date, and text availability
author: davila7
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation files with no executable code. All static findings are false positives: backticks detected are markdown syntax for PubMed query examples, API key placeholders are placeholder text for user configuration, and cryptographic references are mentions in documentation. Users implement their own HTTP requests to official NCBI endpoints.
valueStatement: Finding relevant biomedical research is time-consuming. This skill provides advanced search techniques, MeSH term guidance, and E-utilities API documentation to quickly locate articles, clinical trials, and systematic reviews in PubMed's database of millions of citations.
limitations: Does not execute API calls - users implement their own HTTP requests
No built-in citation management or export functionality
Rate limits enforced by NCBI (3-10 requests per second)
Access limited to PubMed citations - full text depends on publisher

useCases.title: Systematic Literature Reviews
useCases.description: Build comprehensive search strategies using MeSH terms and Boolean operators to find all relevant studies for systematic reviews.

useCases.title: Evidence-Based Practice
useCases.description: Quickly locate recent clinical trials, guidelines, and meta-analyses on treatments and diagnostic methods for patient care.

useCases.title: Research Topic Exploration
useCases.description: Discover published research on your topic, find key authors, and explore citation networks to understand the literature landscape.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Article Search
promptTemplates.scenario: Find recent research on a topic
promptTemplates.prompt: Search PubMed for recent articles on [TOPIC] from 2023-2024. Include systematic reviews and clinical trials.

promptTemplates.title: Clinical Question
promptTemplates.prompt: Find randomized controlled trials comparing [TREATMENT A] versus [TREATMENT B] for [CONDITION] published in the last 5 years.

promptTemplates.title: Author Search
promptTemplates.prompt: Find publications by [AUTHOR NAME] on [TOPIC] in [YEAR]. Include their most cited papers.

promptTemplates.title: Systematic Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Construct a comprehensive PubMed search strategy using PICO framework for [CLINICAL QUESTION]. Include MeSH terms and synonyms.

bestPractices: Use MeSH terms with [mh] tag for consistent, comprehensive coverage of biomedical concepts
Include API key with E-utility requests to increase rate limits from 3 to 10 requests per second
Start broad then refine: begin with general terms, add field tags and filters to narrow results
Use history server (usehistory=y) for queries returning more than 500 articles
antiPatterns: Searching without field tags when precision is needed - automatic term mapping may produce unexpected results
Ignoring rate limits - excessive requests may result in temporary IP blocks from NCBI
Skipping date filters - retrieving thousands of irrelevant older articles instead of recent research
Using general search terms only - misses indexed MeSH terms that provide better coverage

faq.question: Is an API key required for PubMed?
faq.answer: Yes, NCBI requires API keys for E-utility calls. Keys are free and increase rate limits from 3 to 10 requests per second.

faq.question: What is the maximum results per search?
faq.answer: PubMed displays a maximum of 10,000 results. For larger datasets, use the history server with batch retrieval via EFetch.

faq.question: Can I search PubMed from Claude Code?
faq.answer: This skill provides query syntax and API guidance. You implement HTTP calls to NCBI endpoints using requests or similar libraries.

faq.question: Is my search data kept private?
faq.answer: Search queries to NCBI may be logged for service monitoring. No personal data is stored by PubMed beyond standard server logs.

faq.question: Why are some articles not accessible?
faq.answer: PubMed provides citations and abstracts. Full text access depends on publisher availability, institutional subscriptions, or open access status.

faq.question: How does this differ from biopython?
faq.answer: Biopython (Bio.Entrez) provides Python wrappers for E-utilities. This skill focuses on direct REST API usage for custom implementations.
